messages.author: Micah
messages.text: So what's difference between the spread factor of 7 and spread factor of 11 like what I have?
messages.content_html: So what's difference between the spread factor of 7 and spread factor of 11 like what I have?

messages.author: croozer2000
messages.text: Google ai answer. "Spreading Factor (SF) & Coding Rate (CR): These settings work with bandwidth; a higher SF (like 11) improves range and interference resilience but slows things down, while lower values are faster but less robust."
messages.content_html: Google ai answer. "Spreading Factor (SF) & Coding Rate (CR): These settings work with bandwidth; a higher SF (like 11) improves range and interference resilience but slows things down, while lower values are faster but less robust."

messages.author: Micah

messages.content_html: So the spread factors have to match for us to see each other?

messages.author: MYT33.GAMING
messages.text: What’s up everyone. I just installed an Utah county repeater above Provo that’s currently providing coverage across the southern part of Utah county! I just built a 2nd last night that I’ll be testing and have installed within a few days over on the north point of West Mountain. This should even out coverage to the north end of the county!

I just built a 2nd last night that I’ll be testing and have installed within a few days over on the north point of West Mountain. This should even out coverage to the north end of the county!

messages.author: MYT33.GAMING
messages.text: Saw your repeaters pop up. There’s a small group of us trying to get coverage from south Utah county to Logan.

messages.author: swaits
messages.text: Going with non-default settings makes the network pretty limited in terms of discoverability and growth. I agree that MC’s “USA (recommended)” is better suited to higher density, more urban environments. However, I strongly encourage we stick with the defaults anyway. We can definitely fill the gaps with repeaters. I’ve got four more solar nodes I can put out there myself. If we don’t use the defaults, new users have close to zero chance of ever finding the network. They’ll try MC with the defaults, figure out it’s worthless, and give up or go to MT. Please go back to defaults. Let’s try to make it work. I think we are pretty close. @Hunter @pittvypr
messages.content_html: Going with non-default settings makes the network pretty limited in terms of discoverability and growth. I agree that MC’s “USA (recommended)” is better suited to higher density, more urban environments. However, I strongly encourage we stick with the defaults anyway. We can definitely fill the gaps with repeaters. I’ve got four more solar nodes I can put out there myself. If we don’t use the defaults, new users have close to zero chance of ever finding the network. They’ll try MC with the defaults, figure out it’s worthless, and give up or go to MT. Please go back to defaults. Let’s try to make it work. I think we are pretty close. @Hunter @pittvypr

messages.author: Micah
messages.text: I'm glad that I read that, I just pulled out all of my nodes and we're going to switch over, but if we're going to switch back then I'll keep them where they are at. What do you guys think. He does have a point
messages.content_html: I'm glad that I read that, I just pulled out all of my nodes and we're going to switch over, but if we're going to switch back then I'll keep them where they are at. What do you guys think. He does have a point
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Hunter
messages.text: For the record, we aren’t against the defaults or collaborating. But for us, right now, it isn’t working. We need that extra overhead to even get messages through. Once we get the infrastructure built up (band pass filters, 1Watt), I agree that it can work. Currently there isn’t anything to bridge the valleys either, that should be our focus. I agree on the phased approach. Having a broken mesh on default settings can be just as much of a turn off as not seeing anything at all.
messages.content_html: For the record, we aren’t against the defaults or collaborating. But for us, right now, it isn’t working. We need that extra overhead to even get messages through. Once we get the infrastructure built up (band pass filters, 1Watt), I agree that it can work. Currently there isn’t anything to bridge the valleys either, that should be our focus. I agree on the phased approach. Having a broken mesh on default settings can be just as much of a turn off as not seeing anything at all.

1
1

swaits
Going with non-default settings makes the network pretty limited in terms of discoverability and growth. I agree that MC’s “USA (recommended)” is better suited to higher density, more urban environments. However, I strongly encourage we stick with the defaults anyway. We can definitely fill the gaps with repeaters. I’ve got four more solar nodes I can put out there myself. If we don’t use the defaults, new users have close to zero chance of ever finding the network. They’ll try MC with the defaults, figure out it’s worthless, and give up or go to MT. Please go back to defaults. Let’s try to make it work. I think we are pretty close. @Hunter @pittvypr

messages.author: swaits
messages.text: If I handed over the hardware for more solar nodes to you would you reconsider trying the defaults? I think we can make it work! There is enough wilderness, public land, etc around the valleys (plural!) to get great coverage even at the limited SF. And if we do that successfully, we will be rewarded with higher bandwidth and message throughput. I have some boxes just sitting here and haven’t had the time to do them myself. Want them? Just say the word and they’re yours.
messages.content_html: If I handed over the hardware for more solar nodes to you would you reconsider trying the defaults? I think we can make it work! There is enough wilderness, public land, etc around the valleys (plural!) to get great coverage even at the limited SF. And if we do that successfully, we will be rewarded with higher bandwidth and message throughput. I have some boxes just sitting here and haven’t had the time to do them myself. Want them? Just say the word and they’re yours.

messages.author: swaits
messages.text: I’ll add to this that I will, in addition to handing over the nodes I already have here, fund more nodes up to $1k. My thought here is I don’t want to compromise. I want the usability from solid infrastructure AND the discoverability from the default radio settings! So anyone who has the time and wants to take the lead on setting up and maintaining repeaters, please let me know. I’ll give you what I have sitting here and pay for more. Let’s saturate SL valley, UT valley, Davis, Magna, and Toelle. And if that all starts working we can move our way south toward St George and north into Idaho. (edited)
messages.content_html: I’ll add to this that I will, in addition to handing over the nodes I already have here, fund more nodes up to $1k. My thought here is I don’t want to compromise. messages.author: Hunter
messages.text: Saturation is not actually good. It’s better to have fewer, more well placed nodes, instead of throwing random nodes up all over the place. Have you noticed the problem with the 2 characters for the repeater shortname? That gives us only 62 valid repeater ids before getting duplicates. While not fatal, it is annoying. I’d rather have well build filtered 1W nodes than a much of mediocre ones if your serious about throwing around money
messages.content_html: Saturation is not actually good. It’s better to have fewer, more well placed nodes, instead of throwing random nodes up all over the place. Have you noticed the problem with the 2 characters for the repeater shortname? That gives us only 62 valid repeater ids before getting duplicates. While not fatal, it is annoying. I’d rather have well build filtered 1W nodes than a much of mediocre ones if your serious about throwing around money
messages.reply_html:
swaits
I’ll add to this that I will, in addition to handing over the nodes I already have here, fund more nodes up to $1k. My thought here is I don’t want to compromise. I want the usability from solid infrastructure AND the discoverability from the default radio settings! So anyone who has the time and wants to take the lead on setting up and maintaining repeaters, please let me know. I’ll give you what I have sitting here and pay for more. Let’s saturate SL valley, UT valley, Davis, Magna, and Toelle. And if that all starts working we can move our way south toward St George and north into Idaho. (edited)

messages.author: MtnBikeLCC
messages.text: I get that MeshCore is totally open and you are free to experiment with different settings, but it would be nice if we could settle on some radio parameters that most repeaters could adopt along the Wasatch Front so we can build out a strong and consistent mesh. Right now the repeaters across the valley are using a dizzying array of different settings for CR/SF/BW. I'm trying to put up a new repeater at the mouth of LCC and don't know what settings to use. Anyone in charge out there? Any suggestions?
messages.content_html: I get that MeshCore is totally open and you are free to experiment with different settings, but it would be nice if we could settle on some radio parameters that most repeaters could adopt along the Wasatch Front so we can build out a strong and consistent mesh. Right now the repeaters across the valley are using a dizzying array of different settings for CR/SF/BW. I'm trying to put up a new repeater at the mouth of LCC and don't know what settings to use. Anyone in charge out there? Any suggestions?

messages.author: swaits
messages.text: US/Canada recommend setting: 910.525 MHz, 62.5 kHz. SF7, CR5
messages.content_html: US/Canada recommend setting: 910.525 MHz, 62.5 kHz. SF7, CR5

messages.reply_html:
MtnBikeLCC
I tried to get ChatGPT and Claude to collect a table of what all the repeaters are using along the Wasatch Front so I could see (track) what most of them are set to. They were unable to get that info, however. I haven't wanted to do it manually, but I may have to. If anyone has a way to easily collect or export that info it would be great.
